title: Methods and systems for correcting or ameliorating spinal curvature deformities
abstract: Methods and systems for axial spinal derotation for ameliorating or correcting aberrant spinal column deviations, such as but not limited to those associated with scoliosis. The systems and methods help facilitate the application of significant derotational forces to individual vertebrae and posterior elements. The present invention describes a two-piece system comprising a pivot tool with a handle and a saddle reducer, e.g., a temporary rod. The systems herein can replace more complex systems for axial spinal derotation. The systems have the advantage of axial rotation off of one rod instead of two.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A derotation system for correcting a spinal deformity in at least an axial plane, said system comprising: a. a pivot tool (300) comprising: (i) a base (310); (ii) a hook (344) extending from a first position of the base (310); and (iii) a first attachment component extending from a second position of the base (310), the hook (344) is configured to temporarily attach to a rod (210); and b. a saddle reducer (400) comprising an engagement rod (420) configured to temporarily engage pedicle screws and a second attachment component extending from the engagement rod (420), wherein the pivot tool (300) and saddle reducer (400) can be temporarily connected together by engaging the first attachment component with the second attachment component. 2. The system of claim 1 further comprising a handle (320) extending from the base (310) of the pivot tool (300). 3. The system of claim 1 further comprising two handles (320) extending from the base (310) of the pivot tool (300). 4. The system of claim 3, wherein the two handles (320) are at an angle with respect to each other. 5. The system of any of claims 1-4, wherein the hook (344) is connected to the base (310) via a hook extension (340). 6. The system of claim 5, wherein an attachment sleeve (346) is disposed on the hook extension (340) in between the base (310) and the hook (344), the attachment sleeve (346) can move between at least an engaged position and a disengaged position, wherein in the engaged position the attachment sleeve (346) secures the hook (344) to a rod (210) and in the disengaged position the hook (344) can be optionally engaged with or disengaged from the rod (210). 7. The system of any of claims 1 -6, wherein the second attachment component of the saddle reducer (400) is attached to the engagement rod (420) via a base shaft (410). 8. The system of any of claims 1-7, wherein the first position of the base (310) and the second position of the base (310) are spaced a distance d apart. 9. The system of any of claims 1 -8, wherein the first attachment component of the pivot tool (300) comprises a pinhole component (333) adapted to accept a pin, a hook, or a bolt. 10. The system of any of claims 1-8, wherein the second attachment component of the saddle reducer (400) comprises a pinhole (434) adapted to accept a pin, a hook, or a bolt. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein the pinhole component (333) and pinhole (434) can be secured together to connect the pivot tool (300) to the saddle reducer (400). 12. The system of claim 10 or 11 , wherein the second attachment component comprises a slot (432) for accepting the first attachment component, wherein the first attachment component and second attachment component can engage such that the pinhole (434) and pinhole component (333) are aligned. 13. A derotation system for correcting a spinal deformity in at least an axial plane, said system comprising: a pivot tool (300) comprising: (i) a base (310); (ii) a hook (344) extending from a first position of the base (310); and (iii) a first attachment component extending from a second position of the base (310), the hook (344) is configured to temporarily attach to a rod (210). 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the pivot tool (300) can be temporarily connected to a first auxiliary component by engaging a portion of the first auxiliary component with the first attachment component of the pivot tool (300). 15. The system of claim 14, wherein the first auxiliary component is a saddle reducer (400). 16. The system of any of claims 13-15 further comprising a handle (320) extending from the base (310) of the pivot tool (300). 17. The system of any of claims 13-15 further comprising two handles (320) extending from the base (310) of the pivot tool (300). 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the two handles (320) are at an angle with respect to each other. 19. The system of any of claims 13-18, wherein the hook (344) is connected to the base (310) via a hook extension (340). 20. The system of claim 19, wherein an attachment sleeve (346) is disposed on the hook extension (340) in between the base (310) and the hook (344), the attachment sleeve (346) can move between at least an engaged position and a disengaged position, wherein in the engaged position the attachment sleeve (346) securing the hook (344) to a rod (210) and in the disengaged position the hook (344) can be optionally engaged with or disengaged from the rod (210). 21. The system of any of claims 13-20, wherein the first position of the base (310) and the second position of the base (310) are spaced a distance d apart. 22. The system of any of claims 13-21 , wherein the first attachment component of the pivot tool (300) comprises a pinhole component (333) adapted to accept a pin, a hook, or a bolt. 23. A derotation system for correcting a spinal deformity in at least an axial plane, said system comprising: a saddle reducer (400) comprising an engagement rod (420) configured to temporarily engage pedicle screws and a second attachment component extending from the engagement rod (420). 24. The system of claim 23, wherein the saddle reducer (400) can be temporarily connected to a second auxiliary component by engaging a portion of the second auxiliary component with the second attachment component of the saddle reducer. 25. The system of claim 24, wherein the second auxiliary component is a pivot tool (300). 26. The system of any of claims 23-25, wherein the second attachment component of the saddle reducer (400) is attached to the engagement rod (420) via a base shaft (410). 27. The system of any of claims 23-26, wherein the second attachment component of the saddle reducer (400) comprises a pinhole (434) adapted to accept a pin, a hook, or a bolt. 28. The system of any of claims 24-28, wherein the second attachment component comprises a slot (432) for accepting the portion of the second auxiliary component. 29. A kit comprising a derotation system for correcting a spinal deformity in at least an axial plane, said kit comprising: a. a pivot tool (300) comprising: (i) a base (310); (ii) a hook (344) extending from a first position of the base (310); and (iii) a first attachment component extending from a second position of the base (310), the hook (344) is configured to temporarily attach to a rod (210); and b. a saddle reducer (400) comprising an engagement rod (420) configured to temporarily engage pedicle screws and a second attachment component extending from the engagement rod (420), wherein the pivot tool (300) and saddle reducer (400) can be temporarily connected together by engaging the first attachment component with the second attachment component. 30. The kit of claim 29, wherein the pivot tool (300) further comprises a handle (320) extending from the base (310). 31. The kit of claim 29, wherein the pivot tool (300) further comprises two handles (320) extending from the base (310). 32. The kit of claim 31 , wherein the two handles (320) are at an angle with respect to each other. 33. The kit of any of claims 29-32, wherein the hook (344) is connected to the base (310) via a hook extension (340). 34. The kit of claim 33, wherein an attachment sleeve (346) is disposed on the hook extension (340) in between the base (310) and the hook (344), the attachment sleeve (346) can move between at least an engaged position and a disengaged position, wherein in the engaged position the attachment sleeve (346) securing the hook (344) to a rod (210) and in the disengaged position the hook (344) can be optionally engaged with or disengaged from the rod (210). 35. The kit of any of claims 29-34, wherein the second attachment component of the saddle reducer (400) is attached to the engagement rod (420) via a base shaft (410). 36. The kit of any of claims 29-35, wherein the first position of the base (310) and the second position of the base (310) are spaced a distance cf apart. 37. The kit of any of claims 29-36, wherein the first attachment component of the pivot tool (300) comprises a pinhole component (333) adapted to accept a pin, a hook, or a bolt. 38. The kit of any of claims 29-37, wherein the second attachment component of the saddle reducer (400) comprises a pinhole (434) adapted to accept a pin, a hook, or a bolt. 39. The kit of claim 38, wherein the pinhole component (333) and pinhole (434) can be secured together to connect the pivot tool (300) to the saddle reducer (400). 40. The kit of claim 38 or 39, wherein the second attachment component comprises a slot (432) for accepting the first attachment component, wherein the first attachment component and second attachment component can engage such that the pinhole (434) and pinhole component (333) are aligned. 41. A method of correcting or ameliorating a spinal deformity in at least an axial plane, said method comprising: a. temporarily attaching a handle of a derotation system to a rod captured by a first column of pedicle screws; b. temporarily engaging a temporary rod of a derotation system in a second column of pedicle screws opposite the first column; c. temporarily connecting the handle to the temporary rod; and d. applying pressure to the handle of the derotation system and producing downward pressure on the pedicle screws capturing the temporary rod which thereby rotates vertebral bodies and posterior segmental elements to a neutral or normal corrected position. 42. The method of claim 41 , wherein rotation of vertebral bodies is achieved by a single rod placed in pedicle screws of the concave curve. 43. The method of claim 41 or 42, wherein rotation of vertebral bodies does not require bilateral rods. 44. The method of any of claims 41-43, wherein the method is for three-dimensional correction or amelioration of a spinal deformity. 45. The method of any of claims 41-44, wherein the temporary rod is configured to engage polyaxial tulip head pedicle screws. 46. The method of any of claims 41-45 further comprising a. temporarily attaching a handle of a second derotation system to the rod captured by a first column of pedicle screws; b. temporarily engaging a temporary rod of a second derotation system in a second column of pedicle screws opposite the first column; and c. temporarily connecting the handle to the temporary rod. 47. The method of claim 46, wherein the handles of the derotation system and the second derotation system are moved in opposite directions. 48. A method of correcting or ameliorating a spinal deformity in at least an axial plane, said method comprising: a. temporarily attaching a pivot tool (300) to a rod captured by a first column of pedicle screws, the pivot tool (300) comprises a base (310) with a hook (344) disposed thereon in a first position and a first attachment component disposed thereon in a second position, the hook (344) is configured to temporarily attach to the rod captured by the first column of pedicle screws; b. temporarily engaging an engagement rod (420) of a saddle reducer (400) in a second column of pedicle screws opposite the first column, the saddle reducer (400) comprises an engagement rod (420) for temporarily engaging pedicle screws and a second attachment component extending from the engagement rod (420); c. temporarily connecting the pivot tool (300) to the engagement rod by engaging the first attachment component with the second attachment component; and d. applying pressure or force to the pivot tool (300) and producing downward pressure on the pedicle screws capturing the saddle reducer (400), which thereby rotates vertebral bodies and posterior segmental elements to a neutral or normal corrected position. 49. A method of correcting or ameliorating a spinal deformity in at least an axial plane, said method comprising: a. temporarily attaching a handle of a derotation system according to any of claims 1 - 12 to a rod captured by a first column of pedicle screws; b. temporarily engaging an engagement rod of the derotation system in a second column of pedicle screws opposite the first column; c. temporarily connecting the handle to the engagement rod; and d. applying pressure to the handle of the derotation system and producing downward pressure on the pedicle screws capturing the engagement rod, which thereby rotates vertebral bodies and posterior segmental elements to a neutral or normal corrected position. 50. The method of claim 49, wherein rotation of vertebral bodies is achieved by a single rod placed in pedicle screws of the concave curve. 51. The method of claim 49 or 50, wherein rotation of vertebral bodies does not require bilateral rods. 52. The method of any of claims 49-51, wherein the method is for three-dimensional correction or amelioration of a spinal deformity. 53. The method of any of claims 49-52, wherein the temporary rod is configured to engage polyaxial tulip head pedicle screws. 54. The method of any of claims 49-53 further comprising a. temporarily attaching a handle of a second derotation system to the rod captured by a first column of pedicle screws; b. temporarily engaging a temporary rod of a second derotation system in a second column of pedicle screws opposite the first column; and c. temporarily connecting the handle to the temporary rod. 55. The method of claim 54, wherein the handles of the derotation system and the second derotation system are moved in opposite directions.
